violations.description: Multi-step progress indicator has no ARIA attributes (aria-current='step', role='list') to convey step state to screen readers. Users cannot determine which step is active, completed, or pending.
violations.reference_fix:
1. ...
  2. ...
  3. ...

violations.description: Dietary filter tags are <div> elements with cursor:pointer, not <button> or elements with role='checkbox'. They are not keyboard-focusable and cannot be toggled by assistive technology.
violations.reference_fix:
<button class="filter-tag" role="checkbox" aria-checked="true">All</button>
<button class="filter-tag" role="checkbox" aria-checked="false">Vegetarian</button>

violations.description: Quantity increment/decrement buttons show only '−' and '+' symbols with no aria-label. Screen readers cannot distinguish between multiple sets of +/- buttons for different meals.
violations.reference_fix: 0

violations.description: Multiple 'Add' buttons exist with identical text but for different meals. Screen reader users cannot distinguish which meal each Add button corresponds to.
violations.reference_fix: <button class="add-btn" aria-label="Add Herb-Crusted Salmon to order">Add</button>

violations.description: Allergen warning is a plain div with no role='alert' or aria-live. This critical safety information is not announced to screen reader users.

page_description: A meal kit ordering page with multi-step indicator, dietary filters, meal cards with quantity selectors and add buttons, order summary, and allergen warning. Issues include non-semantic step indicator, div-based filter tags, unlabeled quantity buttons, ambiguous add buttons, and unannounced allergen warning.
